meta.context_summary: Requirements elicitation session conducted with minimal stakeholder input provided. System context, boundaries, and operational requirements have been inferred based on standard systems engineering practices.

possible_individuals.name: System Availability
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL maintain 99% operational availability during normal operating conditions
possible_individuals.rationale: High availability is typically required for operational systems

possible_individuals.name: User Interface Requirement
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL provide a user-friendly interface accessible to authorized personnel
possible_individuals.rationale: User interface is essential for system operation and control

possible_individuals.name: Response Time
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL respond to user inputs within 2 seconds under normal load conditions
possible_individuals.rationale: Reasonable response time expectation for user experience

possible_individuals.name: Data Integrity
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL maintain data integrity and prevent unauthorized data modification
possible_individuals.rationale: Data integrity is critical for system reliability and trust

possible_individuals.name: Security Access Control
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL implement role-based access control for all user functions
possible_individuals.rationale: Security is fundamental requirement for most systems

possible_individuals.name: System Monitoring
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL provide real-time monitoring and status reporting capabilities
possible_individuals.rationale: Monitoring is essential for operational awareness and maintenance

possible_individuals.name: Backup and Recovery
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL support automated backup and recovery procedures with RTO of 4 hours
possible_individuals.rationale: Business continuity requires backup and recovery capabilities

possible_individuals.name: Maintenance Access
possible_individuals.description: The system SHALL provide maintenance personnel with diagnostic and configuration access
possible_individuals.rationale: Maintenance access is required for system lifecycle management

classes.name: Normal Operations
classes.description: System operates under standard conditions with all primary functions available to authorized users

classes.name: Primary System Failure
classes.description: Critical system component fails requiring immediate failover or shutdown procedures

classes.name: Scheduled Maintenance
classes.description: Planned maintenance activities performed during designated maintenance windows

classes.name: Reduced Capacity Operation
classes.description: System operates with limited functionality due to partial component failure or maintenance

classes.name: Emergency Shutdown
classes.description: System performs controlled shutdown in response to emergency conditions or operator command

classes.name: System Commissioning
classes.description: Initial system startup and validation procedures following installation or major maintenance

things.name: System Operator
things.description: Primary user responsible for day-to-day system operation and monitoring

things.name: Maintenance Personnel
things.description: Technical staff responsible for system maintenance and repair activities

things.name: System Administrator
things.description: IT personnel responsible for system configuration and user management

things.name: User Interface
things.description: Primary human-machine interface for system interaction and control

things.name: External System Interface
things.description: Communication interface with external systems or networks

things.name: System Boundary
things.description: Logical and physical boundaries defining system scope and responsibilities

system_context.environment: General operational environment with standard infrastructure and support systems
